765	| API / resource | URL | Use in bot | Rate limit | Auth |
766	|---|---|---|---|---|
767	| Polymarket CLOB | `https://clob.polymarket.com` | Orders, market data, tick sizes | 9,000/10s general; POST `/order` 3,500/10s burst, 36,000/10min | L1 + L2 |
768	| CLOB WS market | `wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/market` | Live books for simulator + display | ~5 concurrent WS/IP | None |
769	| CLOB WS user | `wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/user` | OUR own fills/orders | Same | L2 |
770	| Polymarket Gamma | `https://gamma-api.polymarket.com` | Market metadata, slug resolution | 4,000/10s; events 500/10s; markets 300/10s | None |
771	| Polymarket Data API | `https://data-api.polymarket.com` | Leader trades, positions, leaderboard | 1,000/10s; `/trades` 200/10s; `/positions` 150/10s | None |
772	| Data API leaderboard | `https://data-api.polymarket.com/v1/leaderboard` | Daily wallet seeding | included above | None |
773	| Goldsky positions subgraph | `…/positions-subgraph/0.0.7/gn` | Cross-validate positions, time series | Public free | None |
774	| Goldsky orderbook subgraph | `…/orderbook-subgraph/0.0.1/gn` | Historical fills | Public free | None |
775	| Goldsky activity subgraph | `…/activity-subgraph/0.0.4/gn` | Splits/merges/redemptions | Public free | None |
776	| Goldsky OI subgraph | `…/oi-subgraph/0.0.6/gn` | Liquidity gating | Public free | None |
777	| Goldsky PnL subgraph | `…/pnl-subgraph/0.0.14/gn` | Realized PnL for ranking | Public free | None |
778	| The Graph Polymarket | `gateway.thegraph.com/api/<key>/subgraphs/id/Bx1W4S7kDVxs9gC3s2G6DS8kdNBJNVhMviCtin2DiBp` | Backup if Goldsky outage | 100k queries/month free | Free API key |
779	| Alchemy Polygon free | `https://polygon-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/<KEY>` | Primary RPC + log subscription on Exchange V2 | 30 million compute units/month free, 25/s burst | Free API key |
780	| Alchemy WSS | `wss://polygon-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/<KEY>` | `eth_subscribe("logs", …)` | Same bucket | Free API key |
781	| Ankr public Polygon | `https://rpc.ankr.com/polygon` | RPC failover #2 | 200 million API credits/month freemium; 30 RPS public cap | None for public; key optional |
782	| Polygon public RPC | `https://polygon-rpc.com` | RPC failover #3 | Best-effort, no SLA | None |
783	| Chainlist | `https://chainlist.org/chain/137` | Discover more fallbacks | n/a | None |
784	| Polygonscan | `https://api.polygonscan.com/api` | Tx receipts, audit | 5 calls/s free | Free API key |
785	| CoinGecko Free | `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3` | USDC reference price sanity check | 30 calls/min free | None |
786	| Dune Analytics free | `https://api.dune.com/api/v1/query/<id>/results` | Custom cross-validation | 2,500 credits/month (~500 query executions); pay-as-you-go at $5.00 per 100 additional credits beyond the bucket | Free API key |
787	| Polymarket leaderboard HTML | `https://polymarket.com/leaderboard/overall/monthly/profit` | Manual sanity-check | n/a | None |
788	
789	**Optional / secondary (third-party views, not for programmatic ingestion):** `polymarketanalytics.com`, `polymarketrankings.com`, `polycopy.app`, `stand.trade`, `polytrackhq.app`, `merlin.trade/leaderboard`. **Optional Python wrapper:** the community `polymarket-apis` PyPI package (Pydantic-typed Gamma/Data/Subgraph/Web3/WebSocket clients) — viable alternative to building clients from scratch.
790	
791	---
792	
793	### 12. Appendix — decisions explicitly made (no "either/or")
794	
795	| Decision | Choice | Rejected |
796	|---|---|---|
797	| Polymarket SDK | `py-clob-client-v2` | v1 (deprecated April 2026) |
798	| Wallet type | EOA + Gnosis Safe proxy (sigtype 2) | Magic-link (sigtype 1, harder to automate); pure EOA (sigtype 0, must pay gas every trade) |
799	| Detection path | Data API polling + Polygon log subscription (hybrid) | Subgraph-only (too laggy); user WS (only own trades) |
800	| Sizing | Fixed-dollar with rank multiplier | Proportional-to-leader (noisy estimation) |
801	| Order type | FOK market at +2 ticks | GTC limit (adverse-selection risk) |
802	| Aggregation | Single combined order on contemporaneous signals | N separate orders (slippage compounds) |
803	| Conflict resolution | First-mover or higher-score on simultaneous | Vote / average / pick larger leader |
804	| Storage | SQLite WAL + DuckDB for analytics | Postgres (over-engineered locally) |
805	| Paper / live | Two parallel ledger schemas | Single table with mode tag |
806	| Dashboard framework | Next.js + shadcn + Lightweight Charts | Streamlit / Dash / Tauri / SvelteKit |
807	| Logging | structlog + JSON → SQLite | Loki/Grafana (overkill) |
808	| Metrics | SQL on SQLite → JSON | Prometheus + Grafana (overkill) |
809	| Primary RPC | Alchemy Polygon free | Ankr (#2); public Polygon RPC (#3) |
810	| Key storage | OS keychain via `keyring` | Plain `.env` (insufficient); HSM (too heavy) |
811	| Concurrency | asyncio | Threads (GIL); multiprocessing (state-sharing pain) |
812	| Live unlock | 14-day, 400-trade, +3% paper PnL gate | Manual flip (too risky); no gate (irresponsible) |
813	
814	---
815	
816	### 13. What's NOT in scope
817	
818	* Strategy / alpha design — by user mandate, the bot copies, it does not invent.
819	* Market making, arbitrage, RFQ — different domain.
820	* Multi-account fan-out / running for other users.
821	* Tax accounting beyond raw CSV export of fills.
822	* Telegram or mobile notifications — the local dashboard is the only UI.
823	
824	---
825	
826	## Recommendations
827	
828	**Immediate (days 1–7).** Build the data layer first (Phase 1). Specifically: pin `py-clob-client-v2`, stand up the SQLite schema with WAL + migrations, implement read-only clients for the 5 Goldsky subgraphs and the three Polymarket APIs, and run the daily ranking job end-to-end. Verify wash-trading detection on 5 known leaderboard wallets (manually audit each result). **Do not write any order-placement code in this phase** — keep the bot read-only until ranking is trustworthy.
829	
830	**Short term (weeks 2–4).** Phase 2 paper engine. The first integration test that matters is `test_paper_e2e.py`: take a recorded leader trade event, run it through detection → aggregation → sizing → fill simulator → ledger, and assert the full trace chain. The second is `test_crash_recovery.py`: `kill -9` mid-trade, restart, assert zero double-orders.
831	
832	**Medium term (weeks 5–8).** Phase 3 dashboard. Build the Overview + Positions + Live Log views first; everything else is iterative polish. The mode toggle stays disabled in code until Phase 4.
833	
834	**Live unlock decision.** Do not flip to live until ALL §9 criteria are satisfied. If 14 days of paper produces less than +3 % net PnL, that is the data telling you the leader-selection or sizing is not yet good enough; iterate, do not flip. **If you flip to live and the first week diverges from paper by more than 1σ, revert to paper.** This is a feature of the architecture (two-ledger setup means switching is one config flag), not a failure.
835	
836	**Benchmarks that would change recommendations:**
837	
838	* If Polymarket's chain migration is announced with a date, immediately re-evaluate the RPC layer and confirm Alchemy/Ankr support the destination chain. If they don't, the project is paused until they do — chain-only reads are non-negotiable.
839	* If Polymarket deprecates the unauthenticated Data API or the public Goldsky subgraphs, switch to The Graph Network's hosted subgraph (100k queries/month free with API key) and prepare to budget for paid tier if necessary.
840	* If `min_order_size` increases (e.g., to 10 shares), revisit the $5 base sizing — at $5/copy and a 10-share minimum, you'd need outcomes priced ≤ $0.50 only, which biases the bot toward one half of every market.
841	* If the user's bankroll grows beyond $5,000, switch sizing from fixed-dollar to a Kelly-fractional model — at that bankroll, per-trade dollar floors stop dominating.
842	
843	---
844	
845	## Caveats
846	
847	* **The 25 % wash-trading figure refers to the historical aggregate, not necessarily the current period.** The Columbia / Barnard SSRN paper measured the prior three years; the share in any given week could be higher or lower. Treat it as a planning constant, not a real-time signal.
848	* **Latency targets are theoretical.** A 2-second end-to-end target assumes Alchemy's free tier responds in <200 ms and Polymarket's CLOB matches in <600 ms. Both can degrade during high-volume events (sports finals, election nights). The bot's behavior under such conditions is "trade fewer signals, more conservatively"; not "trade harder."
849	* **Polymarket's documented rate limits are confirmed via Cloudflare throttling**, but per-endpoint exact numbers (e.g., `/trades` at 200/10s) are reported by third-party reference guides citing the official rate-limit table; the bot enforces conservative client-side limits well below stated maxima and additionally honors `429` responses with backoff.
850	* **The CLOB V2 platform has been live only since April 28, 2026.** Subtle behavioral changes (e.g., fee at match time vs placement time) may produce unexpected ledger discrepancies relative to pre-V2 documentation written by third parties — verify against the official docs at `docs.polymarket.com` whenever in doubt.
851	* **Profitability is not promised.** The 15.9 %/2 %/0.033 % base rates from on-chain data on 2.5 million addresses tell you the field of which this bot is a member. Copy-trading shifts those odds in your favor only if the underlying leaders are durably skilled — which the daily re-ranking and antigaming layer are designed to maximize but cannot guarantee.
852	* **Polymarket's possible chain migration off Polygon could obsolete parts of this plan.** No timeline has been announced. Keep RPC, contract addresses, and chain ID behind configuration to make the migration a config diff rather than a rewrite.
853	* **Regulatory landscape is mid-flux.** Polymarket's CFTC posture, state-level lawsuits, and international bans are evolving month-to-month in 2026. Verify your jurisdiction's current status before depositing real funds; do not rely on this document's snapshot for legal advice.
854	
855	— End of document.

file.content:
# Polymarket Copy-Trading Bot — Comprehensive Technical Planning Document

**Version:** 1.0 · **Date:** May 24, 2026 · **Status:** Pre-build planning, paper-first

---

## TL;DR

- **Build a Python (`py-clob-client-v2`) + SQLite + FastAPI + Next.js/shadcn/Lightweight-Charts local stack** that copies 20–50 daily-re-ranked Polymarket wallets via a hybrid Data-API-polling + Alchemy-Polygon-log-subscription detection layer, executes through FOK orders sized at $5 base (rank-multiplied $3.50–$7.50, floor $5) to clear the typical per-market `min_order_size` of 5 shares, and uses a single execution function whose only branch on `Mode.PAPER` vs `Mode.LIVE` is whether fills are written by a pessimistic in-memory simulator or by the live CLOB V2 client.
- **Treat paper as live.** All upstream logic — leader ingest, ranking, sizing, aggregation, conflict resolution, exits, capacity caps, kill-switch — is mode-agnostic; live mode is locked in the dashboard until paper validation criteria (14 consecutive days, ≥ 400 trades, > +3% net of simulated slippage, ≤ 8% max daily drawdown, zero ghost trades, zero unrecovered crashes) all hold within the last 14 days.
- **Free APIs are sufficient for everything.** Polymarket's CLOB, Gamma, and Data APIs are public (rates measured in thousands/10s); five Goldsky-hosted subgraphs and Alchemy's free Polygon tier (30M CU/month) cover discovery, ranking and chain-level audit; Dune Analytics' free tier (2,500 credits/month) provides cross-validation; no paid tier of any service is required for a $500-bankroll local bot.

---

## Key Findings

1. **Polymarket migrated to CLOB V2 on April 28, 2026** — new exchange contracts, new collateral token (pUSD), and a fully deprecated V1 SDK; the bot MUST use `py-clob-client-v2` and the V2 endpoints.
2. **The CLOB user-channel websocket cannot subscribe to other users' wallets** — confirmed in Polymarket's documentation. Real-time copy-trading therefore needs a hybrid of (a) Data-API `/trades` polling per leader and (b) Polygon log subscription via Alchemy on the V2 Exchange contract, with Goldsky subgraphs as a reconciliation layer.
3. **The CLOB minimum order size is a per-market field** typically `"5"` (5 shares for limit orders); market orders are constrained at $1 USDC minimum notional. This is the single hardest sizing constraint on a $500 bankroll and is why fixed-dollar sizing (≥ $5/trade) beats proportional-to-leader sizing.
4. **Wash trading is structurally large on Polymarket** — a peer-reviewable study by Columbia and Barnard researchers (Kanoria, Ma, Sethi, Sirolly; SSRN, Nov 2025) found artificial trading averaged 25% of all buying and selling on Polymarket over the prior three years. The discovery layer must therefore include explicit anti-wash/cluster filters; uncritical leaderboard scraping is not safe.
5. **The Polymarket international platform reopened to US users in February 2026** (with state-level carve-outs); the bot does not need to bypass geofencing, but the user must confirm their jurisdiction's status before going live.
6. **The dashboard stack is decided:** Next.js 15 App Router + shadcn/ui + TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 (Apache-2.0, current version 5.2.0). This is the modern dark-trader-terminal pattern (see the `coinpulse` reference build using Next.js 16 + shadcn + Lightweight Charts) and avoids the licensing complexity of TradingView's proprietary Charting Library.
7. **OS-keychain-encrypted secrets, not plain `.env`.** The bot's EOA controls a Safe proxy holding real USDC; loss-of-key is total-loss. The default install reads private key + L2 credentials from the OS keychain via Python's `keyring`; the `.env` carries only references.

---

## Details

### 1. Polymarket platform — state of play as of May 2026

#### 1.1 Architecture

Polymarket is a hybrid-decentralized CLOB on **Polygon PoS (chain ID 137)**. Off-chain matching, on-chain settlement via EIP-712 signed orders against the Exchange smart contract. There is no AMM in the user-facing trading flow today; the legacy FPMM contracts persist for on-chain conversions, splits, and merges, but routine trading is CLOB-only.

**April 28, 2026 CLOB V2 cutover (the dominant fact for this build):**

* Collateral migrated from **USDC.e (bridged) to pUSD (Polymarket USD)** — a Polygon ERC-20 backed 1:1 by Circle USDC, with backing enforced on-chain by the Collateral Onramp contract. The user-visible balance is still labeled USDC; pUSD is wire-level only.
* The Exchange contract was replaced with **CTF Exchange V2** (audited by Cantina and Quantstamp).
* The EIP-712 domain version bumped to `"2"` and the `verifyingContract` is the V2 Exchange.
* **The v1 py-clob-client and v1 TypeScript clob-client no longer work against production.** The v1 PyPI README explicitly directs: *"Please migrate to the V2 client: https://github.com/Polymarket/py-clob-client-v2."*
* All V1 resting orders were wiped during the ~1-hour maintenance window.
* Fees are now charged in pUSD at match time, not in shares at placement.

**Other 2025–2026 changes that matter for a bot:**

* In late April 2026, Polymarket's VP of Engineering DeFi publicly confirmed "chain migration" off Polygon as a roadmap priority; no target chain or date has been set. Build chain identifiers, RPC URLs, and contract addresses as configuration, not constants.
* On Oct 7, 2025, ICE announced a $2B strategic investment; per the official ICE press release, this was at "a valuation of approximately $8 billion pre-investment," and Polymarket CEO Shayne Coplan posted on X the same day that the investment was "valuing us at $9 billion post-money."
* The Polymarket international platform reopened to US users in February 2026; the 2022 CFTC geo-block was lifted. Separate Polymarket US (operated by QCX LLC) runs as the CFTC-regulated entity.
* HeartBeats API and post-only orders launched January 2026 for market-maker resilience.

#### 1.2 The three APIs

| API | Base URL | Purpose | Auth |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CLOB API** | `https://clob.polymarket.com` | Order book + trading + order management | Public reads; L1 (EIP-712) to mint API creds; L2 (HMAC) for trading |
| **Gamma API** | `https://gamma-api.polymarket.com` | Market/event metadata, tags, slugs, sports, search | Public |
| **Data API** | `https://data-api.polymarket.com` | Positions, trades, activity, holders, value, leaderboard | Public |
| **CLOB WebSocket** | `wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/{market\|user}` | Real-time market and user channels | Public market; L2 user |

#### 1.3 CLOB authentication

* **L1 (private key, EIP-712).** Sign the `ClobAuth` struct (`domain.name="ClobAuthDomain"`, `version="1"`, `chainId=137`, message `"This message attests that I control the given wallet"`). Needed only to mint or derive L2 credentials.
* **L2 (apiKey + secret + passphrase, HMAC-SHA256).** Generate once via `client.create_or_derive_api_key()`; every trading request carries `POLY_ADDRESS`, `POLY_SIGNATURE`, `POLY_TIMESTAMP`, `POLY_API_KEY`, `POLY_PASSPHRASE` headers.

#### 1.4 Order types and microstructure (CLOB V2)

* **GTC** (good-till-cancel, resting limit), **GTD** (good-till-date), **FOK** (fill-or-kill — full immediate fill or cancel), **FAK** (fill-and-kill — partial immediate fill, remainder cancelled; replaced what older docs called IOC).
* **Tick sizes** per market: one of `0.1, 0.01, 0.001, 0.0001`. Default $0.01; tightens to $0.001 when an outcome price moves above $0.96 or below $0.04. The bot must read `tick_size` per market and subscribe to the `tick_size_change` WS event — non-conforming orders are rejected.
* **Minimum order size:** per-market field `min_order_size` (typical `"5"` for limit orders); market orders require ≥ $1 USDC notional.
* **Maker fee schedule** is currently 0 bps; market orders pay taker fees per market-specific schedules.

#### 1.5 Rate limits (Cloudflare-throttled, queued not rejected)

| Endpoint family | Burst (10s) | Sustained (10 min) |
|---|---|---|
| General REST | 15,000 | — |
| CLOB general | 9,000 | — |
| CLOB market data (`/book`, `/price`, `/midprice`) single | 1,500 | — |
| CLOB market data batch | 500 | — |
| POST `/order` | 3,500 | 36,000 (~60/s) |
| DELETE `/order` | 3,000 | 30,000 |
| POST `/orders` (batch ≤15) | 1,000 | 15,000 |
| Gamma API general | 4,000 | — |
| Gamma `/events` | 500 | — |
| Gamma `/markets` | 300 | — |
| Gamma `/search` | 350 | — |
| Data API general | 1,000 | — |
| Data API `/trades` | 200 | — |
| Data API `/positions` | 150 | — |

A 50-leader copy bot is multiple orders of magnitude under every limit.

#### 1.6 WebSocket channels

* **`wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/market`** — public; subscribe by `assets_ids` (token IDs). Emits `book`, `price_change`, `tick_size_change`, `last_trade_price`. With `custom_feature_enabled: true`: also `best_bid_ask`, `new_market`, `market_resolved`. Server sends ping every 5s; client must reply pong within 10s. Soft cap of approximately 5 concurrent WS connections per IP.
* **`wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/user`** — L2-authenticated; subscribe by `markets` (condition IDs). Emits `trade` and `order` events for **your own** account only. It does NOT report other users' trades — a critical architectural constraint: leader detection must use the market channel or Polygon logs, not the user channel.

#### 1.7 Data API — endpoints used by the bot

All public, no auth. Base `https://data-api.polymarket.com`. Accepts both EOA and proxy addresses in `user`; responses always return `proxyWallet`.

* **`GET /positions?user=<addr>`** — paginated; `limit` default 100, max 500; `sortBy ∈ {TOKENS, CURRENT, INITIAL, CASHPNL, PERCENTPNL, TITLE, RESOLVING, PRICE}`; filters `market` (conditionId CSV), `sizeThreshold`, `redeemable`, `mergeable`, `title`.
* **`GET /trades?user=<addr>`** — paginated DESC; filters `market`, `side`, `takerOnly` (default true), `filterType` (CASH|TOKENS), `filterAmount`.
* **`GET /activity?user=<addr>`** — TRADE / SPLIT / MERGE / REDEEM / REWARD / CONVERSION events with `start`/`end` unix-seconds windows.
* **`GET /holders?market=<conditionId>`** — top holders.
* **`GET /value?user=<addr>`** — total USD value of open positions.
* **`GET /v1/leaderboard`** — official leaderboard. Params: `category` (OVERALL, POLITICS, SPORTS, CRYPTO, CULTURE, MENTIONS, WEATHER, ECONOMICS, TECH, FINANCE), `timePeriod` (DAY, WEEK, MONTH, ALL), `orderBy` (PNL, VOL), `limit` (default 25, max 50), `offset` (max 1000). Returns `[{rank, proxyWallet, userName, vol, pnl, profileImage, xUsername, verifiedBadge}]`. This is the seed for daily wallet discovery.

#### 1.8 Goldsky subgraphs (public, no auth, GraphQL POST)

Base: `https://api.goldsky.com/api/public/project_cl6mb8i9h0003e201j6li0diw/subgraphs/<name>/<version>/gn`.

| Subgraph | Path | Use in bot |
|---|---|---|
| Positions | `positions-subgraph/0.0.7/gn` | Cross-check Data API, time series |
| Orderbook / Orders | `orderbook-subgraph/0.0.1/gn` | Historical fills, last-trade-price |
| Activity | `activity-subgraph/0.0.4/gn` | Splits/merges/redemptions audit |
| Open Interest | `oi-subgraph/0.0.6/gn` | Liquidity gating |
| PnL | `pnl-subgraph/0.0.14/gn` | Authoritative realized PnL for ranking |

The Graph also hosts a combined subgraph at `gateway.thegraph.com/api/<key>/subgraphs/id/Bx1W4S7kDVxs9gC3s2G6DS8kdNBJNVhMviCtin2DiBp` (100k queries/month free with a Graph API key) as a backup if Goldsky has an outage.

#### 1.9 The proxy-wallet / funder model — critical

Polymarket positions and pUSD balances never live on the EOA. Every Polymarket user has a proxy wallet:

* **Magic-link (email) users** → CREATE2-derived **EIP-1167 minimal proxy** (signature type **1**).
* **Browser / MetaMask / external-EOA users** → **modified Gnosis Safe (1-of-1 multisig)** (signature type **2**).
* **Pure EOA-signed trading** (deposit wallets, API-only setups) → signature type **0**.

All ERC-1155 outcome tokens and pUSD balances live on the **proxy address** (the `funder`), not the EOA. APIs report trades against `proxyWallet`. Proxy factory address: `0x56C79347e95530c01A2FC76E732f9566dA16E113`.

**Copy-trading implications:**
1. The "leader wallet" you track is the **proxy address** (the 0x address on the leaderboard, profile URL, and `proxyWallet` field). Do not follow their EOA — it will appear inactive.
2. The bot's own trades emit under *its* proxy address; the `funder` parameter you pass to `py-clob-client-v2` is your proxy.
3. To recover the canonical proxy from an EOA, use the deterministic CREATE2 derivation; `py-clob-client-v2` and `rs-clob-client` ship helpers (`derive_safe_wallet`, `derive_proxy_wallet`).

#### 1.10 Wallet setup for the bot (decisive)

**Use a fresh dedicated EOA + Gnosis Safe proxy (signature type 2).**

1. Generate a new EOA locally (`eth_account.Account.create()`); store the private key only in the OS keychain (see §10), not in plain `.env`.
2. Send ~$3 of POL to the EOA for gas insurance (allowance-setting, manual wrapping, withdrawals).
3. Deploy the Safe proxy by the first interaction with Polymarket, or proactively via the Safe factory.
4. Bridge / deposit USDC on Polygon to the **Safe proxy address** (NOT the EOA). First trade auto-wraps USDC → pUSD via the Collateral Onramp.
5. Run a one-shot `set_allowances.py` (adapted from NautilusTrader's adapter or the official `agent-skills` repo) to grant max allowance to Exchange V2 and CTF on pUSD and the 1155 tokens.
6. Call `client.create_or_derive_api_key()` once; store `{apiKey, secret, passphrase}` in the OS keychain.

Per-trade gas cost is essentially zero in USDC terms because Polymarket's Builder Relayer gas-stations CLOB matches; POL is held only as insurance for direct contract calls.

#### 1.11 Geographic restrictions — honest note

The international platform is open to US users again as of February 2026; the 2022 CFTC block was lifted. It remains blocked in OFAC-sanctioned jurisdictions (Iran, North Korea, Cuba, Russia, Syria) and a growing list of others including France, Belgium, Singapore, Portugal, Hungary, Argentina, and the UK (gambling-licensing actions). A separate **Polymarket US** entity runs as the CFTC-regulated platform. The bot does not bypass geofencing; verify your specific jurisdiction and contract type before going live, and respect ToS.

---

### 2. Wallet discovery & ranking

#### 2.1 Objective

Maintain a daily-refreshed roster of 20–50 "fast" wallets — high trade frequency, short hold times, small/medium per-trade sizes — re-computed every day at 04:00 UTC and persisted with full diff history.

#### 2.2 Sources, ranked

1. **Data API `/v1/leaderboard`** — primary seed. Pull DAY, WEEK, MONTH × OVERALL/POLITICS/SPORTS/CRYPTO. With `limit=50, offset=0,50,…,500`, ~500 wallets/category/window. ~30 requests/day.
2. **Goldsky `pnl-subgraph`** — authoritative 30-day realized PnL ranking.
3. **Goldsky `orderbook-subgraph` + `activity-subgraph`** — trade counts, hold times, market diversity.
4. **Data API `/trades` + `/positions` per candidate** — drill-down.
5. **Data API `/value`** — current open-positions $ for the size band filter.
6. **Public Dune dashboards (free tier, 2,500 credits/month; pay-as-you-go at $5.00 per 100 additional credits beyond the bucket; ~500 query executions/month free)** — cross-validation:
   * `dune.com/rchen8/polymarket`
   * `dune.com/filarm/polymarket-activity`
   * `dune.com/datadashboards/polymarket-overview`
   * `dune.com/defioasis/polymarket-pnl`
   * `dune.com/genejp999/polymarket-leaderboard`
   * `dune.com/lujanodera/polymarket-analysis`
7. **Third-party analytics (cross-check only):** `polymarketanalytics.com/traders`, `polymarketrankings.com`, `polycopy.app`, `stand.trade`, `polytrackhq.app`, `merlin.trade/leaderboard`. All scrape the Polymarket APIs.
8. **Alchemy Polygon free RPC** — direct chain queries for ground-truth and cluster detection.
9. **Ankr + public `polygon-rpc.com`** — failover RPCs.

#### 2.3 Discovery funnel (daily)

```
Step 1 — Seed (Data API + Goldsky pnl-subgraph):
    Top 500 wallets by 7-day PNL (Data API leaderboard)
    Top 500 wallets by 30-day PNL (Goldsky pnl-subgraph)
    Top 200 by VOL on each of DAY/WEEK/MONTH (Data API)
    Union ≈ 1,200-1,500 candidate proxy addresses.

Step 2 — Hard filters (drop immediately):
    - 30d trade count < 30           → statistical noise
    - 30d trade count > 5,000        → likely MM bot, not "fast directional"
    - Current /value > $250,000      → too large; your $5 copies are noise; their fills move price
    - Current /value < $1,000        → too small / dust-farmer
    - First-trade timestamp < 21d ago → insufficient history
    - Wash-trading score > 0.3       → drop (see §2.5)
    - Cluster sibling already in roster → drop dupes (see §2.5)

Step 3 — Score remaining ≈ 200 candidates (see §2.4).

Step 4 — Top 50 by score: top 30 "active", 31-50 "standby".
```

#### 2.4 Ranking score — exact formula

For each wallet over a 30-day rolling window:

```
trades_30d        = taker trades from /trades
trade_freq        = trades_30d / 30
median_hold_hours = median(close_ts - open_ts) per closed position
win_rate          = closed_winners / closed_trades
realized_pnl_30d  = sum realized PnL on closed trades (pnl-subgraph)
avg_position_usd  = mean(initialValue) of opens in window
per_trade_pnl     = realized_pnl_30d / closed_trades_30d
per_trade_pnl_std = stddev of per-trade realized PnL
sharpe_like       = per_trade_pnl / per_trade_pnl_std       (clip to [-3, 3])
market_diversity  = unique conditionIds / trades_30d         (clip to [0, 1])
recent_7d_pnl     = realized PnL over last 7 days
```

Percentile-normalize each within the candidate pool, then:

```
score = 0.25 * pct(trade_freq)              # FAST = trade often
      + 0.20 * pct(sharpe_like)             # consistent per-trade edge
      + 0.15 * pct(win_rate)
      + 0.15 * pct(recent_7d_pnl)           # recent form
      + 0.10 * (1 - pct(median_hold_hours)) # SHORT holds preferred
      + 0.10 * pct(market_diversity)        # not a one-market specialist
      + 0.05 * pct(realized_pnl_30d)        # de-emphasized at small bankroll
```

Tie-breaks: higher `trades_30d`, then later `last_trade_ts`. Top 30 = active, 31–50 = standby.

#### 2.5 Survivorship-bias and anti-gaming guardrails

* Minimum 30 closed trades, minimum 21-day account age, positive realized 30d PnL, max 35% 30d drawdown, multiplicative 0.5× penalty when `last_trade_ts > 48h`.
* **Wash-trading score (drop if > 0.3)** is a weighted sum of:
  1. Counterparty concentration (HHI > 0.4 in any 24h window over `/activity` + `orderFilledEvent`s) → +0.5.
  2. Round-trip clustering: buy-then-sell of `(conditionId, outcomeIndex)` within ≤10 min netting near-zero share-balance change; > 15% of trades → +0.3.
  3. Extreme-price trades (price < $0.01 or > $0.99) > 20% of trades → +0.4.
  4. Volume/PnL imbalance: `|realized_pnl_30d| / total_volume_30d < 0.001` at volume > $50k → +0.4.
* **Cluster detection by funding-source analysis.** For each candidate's controlling EOA (resolved by reading the Safe singleton's owner via Polygon RPC), trace USDC funding hops via `eth_getLogs`. Edge: "EOA A funded EOA B with ≥ $100 USDC within 7 days of B's first trade." In any weakly-connected component, keep only the highest-scoring wallet.

These guardrails are not theoretical: the Columbia / Barnard paper *"Network-Based Detection of Wash Trading"* (SSRN, Nov. 6, 2025) by Yash Kanoria and Hongyao Ma (Columbia Business School), Rajiv Sethi (Barnard College), and doctoral student Allen Sirolly states that "the 'artificial trading'…accounted for an average of 25% of all buying and selling on Polymarket over the past three years." Treat naive leaderboard scraping as poisoned.

#### 2.6 Cold-wallet rule (intra-day)

Pause copying from a wallet (without removing from the roster):
* Last detected trade > 24 h ago.
* 7-day rolling realized PnL turns negative AND drawdown > 15% from 30d peak.
* Three consecutive copied trades from this leader closed at a loss in last 48 h.

Resume automatically when conditions reverse.

### 3. Copy execution engine

#### 3.1 Detection — hybrid

Because the CLOB user WebSocket cannot subscribe to *other users'* wallets, the bot uses:

* **(a) Polygon log subscription via Alchemy `eth_subscribe("logs", …)`** on the V2 Exchange contract's `OrderFilled` / `OrdersMatched` events, filtered for active leader proxy addresses in `maker`/`taker`. Polygon block propagation is ~2s; the event reaches the bot within ~50–200 ms of inclusion. Alchemy's free tier covers a continuous Polygon log subscription comfortably.
* **(b) Polling `data-api.polymarket.com/trades?user=<wallet>&limit=20` per leader on a 3 s stagger.** Data API picks up matches ~500 ms after off-chain match — *before* chain settlement. **Primary signal path.** Thirty leaders × every 3 s = 10 req/s, well under the 200/10s `/trades` limit.
* **(c) Goldsky `orderbook-subgraph` polling at 10 s** as a reconciliation/audit layer, ~5–30 s lagged.

**Latency budget (leader fill → bot order submitted):**

| Stage | Typical | Worst case |
|---|---|---|
| Leader fill matched off-chain | t = 0 | t = 0 |
| Data API `/trades` updated | +300 ms | +900 ms |
| Bot poll picks up new trade | +1,500 ms (3 s poll avg) | +3,000 ms |
| Decision logic (dedup, sizing, risk) | +20 ms | +100 ms |
| Order signing (EIP-712) | +30 ms | +80 ms |
| POST `/order` to CLOB | +150 ms | +600 ms |
| **End-to-end** | **~2 s** | **~4.7 s** |

When the log-subscription path (a) fires first (often once the chain settlement is included), end-to-end can be ~600–800 ms. **Two seconds is the realistic target.** Sub-second is the aspiration.

#### 3.2 Sizing — decisive

**Fixed-dollar with leader-rank multiplier**, NOT proportional-to-leader-bankroll. A leader's bankroll cannot be reliably estimated, and copying their *percentage* on $500 produces $0.50 trades that violate the per-market `min_order_size` of 5 shares.

```
PER_TRADE_BASE_USD   = 5.00
SIZE_RANK_MULTIPLIER = {
   ranks 1-10  : 1.50  ($7.50)
   ranks 11-20 : 1.00  ($5.00)
   ranks 21-30 : 0.70  ($3.50, floor enforced below)
}
MIN_NOTIONAL_USD     = 5.00
MAX_NOTIONAL_USD     = 10.00
SLIPPAGE_BUFFER      = 0.02  # cap effective impact at 2% of leader's fill
```

Per copy: `size_usd = clip(PER_TRADE_BASE_USD * SIZE_RANK_MULTIPLIER[rank], MIN, MAX)`. Shares = `floor(size_usd / p_leader)`; reject if `shares < market.min_order_size`. Clamp price source to `min(p_leader, top_of_book_ask)` to handle the p ≥ $1 edge case.

**Why this works on $500:** Max 25 concurrent positions (§3.7) × $5–$7 average = $125–$175 allocated, leaving 65 %+ free for new signals. Average copy is 5–10 shares, comfortably above the 5-share floor.

#### 3.3 Aggregation: multiple leaders, same market

When ≥ 2 leaders enter the **same (conditionId, outcomeIndex)** within a **120 s rolling window**:

* The first signal opens the position at chosen size.
* Each subsequent signal in the window from a *different* leader adds 50 % of base sizing, capped at `MAX_NOTIONAL_USD × 3 = $30` total exposure per market.
* The position is tagged with all contributing leaders; PnL attribution is split proportionally.

**Do not** open separate orders per leader for the same market — that triples slippage. Single `clientOrderId` per aggregated entry.

#### 3.4 Routing — decisive

**FOK market-style orders priced at `min(leader_fill_price + 2 ticks, top_of_book_ask)`.** Reasoning:

* GTC limits at the leader's exact fill price are appealing (zero maker fee) but in fast markets frequently fail to fill before the edge decays — adverse selection is the dominant copy-trading risk.
* FOK guarantees fill at predictable slippage or no trade.
* Polymarket's maker fee schedule is currently 0 bps, so the financial incentive to be a maker is small.
* Sells (exits): same FOK logic at `max(leader_exit_price - 2 ticks, top_of_book_bid)`.

If FOK fails (book moved past +2 ticks during submission), do not retry — log and move on; the signal has decayed.

#### 3.5 Exit logic

Each position carries `{leader_proxies, market_id, outcome, entry_ts, entry_size, entry_price}`. Continuous triggers:

1. **Leader-exit mirror.** Any contributing leader scales out ≥ 50 % of their position → bot sells the same fractional %. When aggregated leader exposure hits zero → fully close.
2. **Time stop.** `now - entry_ts > 24 h` AND no leader has updated their position in 12+ h → close at market.
3. **Market-resolution stop.** When `time_to_resolution < 6 h`, freeze sizing (no new opens), exit any existing position with unrealized PnL > +5 %. Resolution risk is unmodelable.
4. **Hard PnL stops.** Per-position stop-loss at −40 % of notional; daily aggregate loss > 8 % of bankroll triggers kill-switch.

#### 3.6 Conflict resolution: YES vs NO

* No current position: take the **first** signal.
* Already hold YES, new NO signal: ignore NO. The thesis is owned by the held position.
* Both signals in the same 120 s window: take the **higher-scoring leader's** side. Log rejected signal with full reasoning.

#### 3.7 Capacity limits ($500 bankroll)

| Limit | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Max concurrent open positions | 25 | Diversification floor, room for new signals |
| Max exposure per single market | $30 (≈ 6 % bankroll) | One resolution failure capped at 6 % |
| Max exposure per single leader (sole contributor) | $50 (10 %) | Concentration cap |
| Max daily new openings | 40 trades | Spread/fee budget cap |
| Daily loss kill-switch | −8 % ($40 from session start) | Stop the bleed, force review |
| Per-position stop-loss | −40 % of notional | ~$2 max single-position loss |

#### 3.8 Idempotency and crash recovery

Every order carries a deterministic `clientOrderId = sha256(leader_proxy + market_id + outcome + leader_fill_tx_hash + bot_size_bucket)[:16]`. SQLite `attempted_orders` table is written **before** POST. On recovery:

1. Load rows with `status IN ('pending', 'submitted')`.
2. Query `client.get_order(server_order_id)` or `/trades?user=<my_proxy>&limit=…` filtered by token+price.
3. Reconcile: matched on-chain → FILLED, populate positions; still open → keep tracking; timed out > 5 min → CANCELED.
4. Re-arm signal processing only after reconciliation completes.

A `kill -9` mid-trade cannot result in a double order.

### 4. Paper trading mode

#### 4.1 Single code path

```python
def execute_order(intent: OrderIntent, mode: Mode):
    # ... all upstream logic identical ...
    if mode is Mode.LIVE:
        return clob_client.create_and_post_order(...)
    return paper_fill_engine.simulate(intent, current_book)
```

The mode is a single config flag; the dashboard shows it as a giant header badge (red PAPER / green LIVE).

#### 4.2 Realistic simulator

The simulator subscribes to the same `wss://…/ws/market` channel and maintains an in-memory L2 book per active market. On `simulate(intent)`:

1. Snapshot the book at intent time.
2. For a market BUY FOK with notional `N`, walk asks from best ask upward, consuming size until `N` is filled or book exhausts.
3. **Pessimism:** apply +0.5-cent worst-side adjustment to the VWAP fill price (your real order would move the book by up to one tick), AND drop 25 % of visible size at each level (other takers + latency exist). Paper fills are slightly *worse* than reality — desirable conservatism.
4. Simulated fees: 0 bps current; engine retains a `paper_fee_rate_bps` config for what-if testing.
5. Write a `paper_fill` row with `(client_order_id, fill_price, fill_size, fees_usd, slippage_bps, book_snapshot_id)`.

FOK that the simulator decides would fail (insufficient depth within +2 ticks) returns `status=REJECTED` and opens no position.

#### 4.3 PnL, fees, slippage tracking

Three accumulating tables: `paper_positions` / `live_positions` (parallel schemas), `pnl_events(ts, mode, position_id, realized_delta, unrealized_delta, reason)`, and `cost_breakdown(ts, mode, position_id, gross_pnl, fees_usd, slippage_usd, net_pnl)` where `slippage_usd = (fill_price - mid_price_at_intent) * size`.

The dashboard shows both gross and net-of-fees+slippage PnL.

#### 4.4 Data structure — decisive

**Two parallel ledgers** (`paper_*` and `live_*` tables with identical schemas), NOT a single ledger with a `mode` tag. Forces zero cross-contamination, simplifies SQL, makes paper↔live a clean cutover, and allows running paper indefinitely alongside live for ongoing validation.

```
paper_trades / live_trades        # every order intent, attempt, outcome
paper_positions / live_positions  # open and historical positions
paper_fills / live_fills          # granular per-fill records
events                            # single shared log with mode tag
leader_signals                    # mode-independent
leader_rankings                   # daily roster snapshot
```

### 5. Logging & observability

#### 5.1 Stack — chosen

`structlog` 24.x + stdlib `logging` JSONFormatter, writing to (a) stdout, (b) a daily-rotating file, (c) a SQLite `events` table the dashboard reads directly. No Loki/Grafana — overkill for a single-user local bot.

#### 5.2 Logged events (categories)

Every event: `ts, trace_id, span_id, level, event_name, mode, source, payload, parent_trace_id?`.

* **Leader detection:** `leader_trade_detected`.
* **Ranking:** `leader_roster_built`, `leader_score_changed`, `wallet_added`, `wallet_dropped`, `wallet_clustered_with`.
* **Decision:** `signal_received`, `signal_dedup_skipped`, `signal_aggregated`, `signal_rejected` (with reason), `sizing_applied`.
* **Order lifecycle:** `order_built`, `order_signed`, `order_submitted`, `order_accepted`, `order_filled`, `order_partial_fill`, `order_canceled`, `order_rejected`.
* **Positions:** `position_opened`, `position_increased`, `position_decreased`, `position_closed`.
* **PnL:** `pnl_realized`, `pnl_unrealized_snapshot` (every 30 s).
* **System:** `websocket_connected`, `websocket_disconnected` (with code/reason), `websocket_reconnect_attempt`, `rate_limit_hit`, `rpc_error`, `subgraph_lag_detected`, `bot_started`, `bot_stopped`, `mode_switched`.
* **Errors:** `exception_caught` (full traceback), `recovery_started`, `recovery_completed`, `kill_switch_triggered`.

#### 5.3 Trace IDs

When `leader_trade_detected` fires, a UUIDv7 (time-sortable) `trace_id` is created. All downstream events inherit it via `structlog.contextvars`. By the time `order_filled` fires, every intermediate decision is queryable as `SELECT * FROM events WHERE trace_id = ?`.

#### 5.4 Storage

* **SQLite (WAL mode)** is the operational store. Settings: `journal_mode=WAL`, `synchronous=NORMAL`, `cache_size=-65536`, `mmap_size=268435456`. Daily VACUUM after the 04:00 UTC ranking job.
* **DuckDB** (with the `sqlite_scanner` extension) for analytical queries on the events table — no copy needed. The dashboard's log explorer and analytics views use DuckDB.

#### 5.5 Metrics

**Direct SQLite queries from the dashboard.** FastAPI's `GET /metrics` returns canned aggregations as JSON. Dashboard polls every 5 s for top-line; live events fan out via WebSocket.

### 6. Dashboard — the cool one

#### 6.1 Stack — decisive

**Next.js 15 (App Router) + React + TypeScript + Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui + TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 + TanStack Query + a single FastAPI WebSocket for live push.**

Not Streamlit, not Dash, not Tauri, not SvelteKit. Reasoning:
* Next.js + shadcn is the modern dark-trader-terminal aesthetic.
* The `coinpulse` reference (Next.js 16 + shadcn + TradingView Lightweight Charts) is precisely this pattern.
* TradingView Lightweight Charts v5 (current version 5.2.0, Apache-2.0 license, ~190 KB bundle size) avoids the licensing complexity of TradingView's proprietary Charting Library. Per Socket.dev's npm package analysis: "The npm package lightweight-charts receives a total of 263,815 weekly downloads," confirming ecosystem support.
* Tauri/Electron add packaging complexity for no benefit when the dashboard always runs locally.

#### 6.2 Topology

```
   ┌───────────────┐    ws + REST     ┌────────────────────┐
   │   Browser     │ ←──────────────→ │ FastAPI :8787      │
   │ Next.js :3000 │                  │ control plane      │
   └───────────────┘                  └────────────────────┘
                                              │
                                              ▼
                                       ┌────────────────┐
                                       │ SQLite (WAL)   │
                                       │ bot.db         │
                                       └────────────────┘
                                              ▲
                                              │
                                       ┌────────────────┐
                                       │ Bot process    │
                                       │ (asyncio)      │
                                       └────────────────┘
```

Both processes read/write the SQLite file (WAL handles concurrency). The bot publishes events into a `events_outbox` table polled at 100 ms by FastAPI, which fans events out to the browser via a single `/ws` connection.

#### 6.3 Dashboard views

* **A. Overview** — mode badge, equity curve (Lightweight Charts line series), today's gross/net PnL, trade count, win rate, hit rate, max drawdown today, bankroll free vs allocated, kill-switch progress.
* **B. Positions** — live table `market | outcome | leader(s) | entry | mark | size | unrealized $ | unrealized % | age | exit state`. Row click → market chart with overlay of bot entry/scale-ins/exits and contributing leaders' entries/exits on the same time axis.
* **C. Closed positions ledger** — filterable, with realized PnL, fees, slippage breakdown, per-leader attribution.
* **D. Leader leaderboard** — current 30 active + 20 standby, daily/weekly stats, score, score delta vs yesterday, contribution to bot PnL, enable/disable toggle per leader, cold indicator.
* **E. Markets** — markets the bot is in, time-to-resolution, depth chart (shadcn-styled).
* **F. Live log feed** — streaming via WS, filterable by category/leader/market/trace ID/level. Click trace → vertical timeline from `leader_trade_detected` → `order_filled`.
* **G. Daily re-ranking results** — diff vs yesterday: wallets added/dropped, rank ≥ 5 changes, score ≥ 0.1 changes; sparklines of each wallet's 30 d PnL.
* **H. System health** — WS status (CLOB market, log subscription, FastAPI↔browser), rolling-window rate-limit usage, last leader trade detected per leader, error counts by category, process uptime.
* **I. Settings** — pause/resume, kill switch, per-leader toggles, mode toggle (paper↔live) gated behind a "type ENABLE LIVE" modal AND the §9 validation precondition.
* **J. Analytics** — equity curve, 7-d rolling win rate, trade frequency, hold-time distribution histogram, slippage analysis (paper vs live deltas), per-leader PnL contribution waterfall.

#### 6.4 Aesthetic

Dark theme by default. JetBrains Mono for tickers. shadcn/ui throughout; no custom CSS for primitives. Bloomberg-style information density. Live counters animate via Framer Motion.

### 7. Tech stack — final, decisive

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Bot language | Python 3.11 | `py-clob-client-v2` is official |
| Async runtime | `asyncio` + `httpx` (HTTP) + `websockets` (raw WS) + `gql` (GraphQL) | Standard, well-supported |
| Polymarket SDK | **`py-clob-client-v2`** (V1 deprecated April 2026) | Official |
| EVM access | `web3.py` 7.x | Polygon RPC + log subscription |
| Process supervisor | `honcho` (Procfile) for dev; `supervisord` or systemd user unit for prod | Lightweight; no Docker needed |
| Database | SQLite 3.45+ (WAL) | Zero-ops, ACID, perfect for single-user |
| Analytical queries | DuckDB 1.x with `sqlite_scanner` | Fast aggregations over SQLite |
| Logging | `structlog` 24.x + stdlib `logging` JSONFormatter | Structured, trace IDs |
| Control plane | FastAPI + uvicorn (`127.0.0.1:8787`) | REST + WS, trivial DX |
| Dashboard framework | Next.js 15 (App Router) + TypeScript | De-facto modern stack |
| UI components | shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4 + Lucide icons | Free "cool" aesthetic |
| Charts | **TradingView Lightweight Charts v5.2.0** (Apache-2.0) | Smallest/fastest financial charts; no license complexity |
| Data fetching | TanStack Query v5 + custom WS hook | Cache + invalidation discipline |
| Animations | Framer Motion | Smooth number tickers |
| Type validation | Pydantic v2 | Runtime safety on all API responses |
| Secrets | OS keychain via `keyring` Python | macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Linux Secret Service |
| Dependency mgmt | `uv` (Python); `pnpm` (JS) | Fastest installs in 2026 |

#### 7.1 Bot ↔ dashboard wire protocol

* REST: `GET /api/state`, `GET /api/positions`, `GET /api/leaders`, `POST /api/settings/{key}`, `POST /api/kill-switch`, `POST /api/mode` (gated).
* WebSocket: single `/ws` pushing JSON typed by `kind`: `event`, `position_update`, `pnl_tick`, `leader_signal`, `system_health`.

#### 7.2 Deployment (local)

```bash
# install
git clone <repo> polymarket-bot && cd polymarket-bot
uv venv && uv pip install -e ".[dev]"        # backend
cd dashboard && pnpm install                  # frontend
cp .env.example .env                          # fill keys; see §10
python -m bot.scripts.bootstrap               # mint CLOB API keys, set allowances, smoke test
# run
honcho start                                  # bot + fastapi
cd dashboard && pnpm dev                      # → http://localhost:3000
```

For always-on machines, wrap with `supervisord` or a systemd user unit (documented in `OPS.md`).

│   │   │   ├── LiveLog.tsx
│   │   │   └── …
│   │   ├── lib/
│   │   │   ├── api.ts
│   │   │   └── ws.ts
│   │   └── hooks/
│   └── README.md
│
└── tests/
    ├── unit/
    ├── integration/
    │   └── test_paper_e2e.py            # full paper-trade lifecycle replay
    └── fixtures/
```

---

### 9. Phased build plan

#### Phase 1 — Data layer + wallet discovery & ranking (read-only)

**Goal:** Daily discovery job producing a defensible roster, no ability to place orders.

* Repo scaffolded, `uv` env, pre-commit, `ruff` + `pytest` CI.
* Pydantic settings + populated `.env.example`.
* SQLite migrations: `leader_candidates`, `leader_rosters`, `events`, `cluster_graph`.
* Clients: Gamma, Data API, 5 Goldsky subgraphs, Polygon RPC (Alchemy primary, Ankr + public fallback).
* Daily job: leaderboard pull, candidate union, hard filters, scoring, cluster detection, roster persistence.
* CLI `dump_roster.py` prints today's roster with reasoning.
* **Done when:** `python -m bot.discovery.job` runs cleanly twice in a row, produces 30–50 wallets with audit trail; integration test replays a fixed leader through the wash-trading detector and produces the expected score.

#### Phase 2 — Paper trading engine

**Goal:** End-to-end paper copies with realistic fill simulation.

* WS market-channel client with reconnect/heartbeat; in-memory L2 book per market.
* Polygon log subscription on Exchange V2 with leader-proxy filter.
* Data API `/trades` polling per leader (3 s stagger).
* Signal dedup, aggregation, sizing, routing.
* Paper fill engine with pessimism adjustment.
* Exit logic, conflict resolution, capacity limits.
* Crash-recovery reconciliation tested with `kill -9` mid-trade.
* Structured logging with trace IDs end-to-end.
* **Done when:** Bot runs 7 consecutive days paper, ≥ 200 simulated trades, every copy has a complete trace chain in `events`, and replaying any trace ID in pure code reproduces the same decision.

#### Phase 3 — Dashboard

**Goal:** All 10 views (§6.3) wired and live.

* Next.js 15 + Tailwind + shadcn scaffold, dark theme.
* FastAPI control plane (REST + WS fanout).
* Overview (equity curve via Lightweight Charts) + Positions + Closed ledger.
* Leader leaderboard + Markets + Live log.
* Re-ranking diff page + System health.
* Analytics page (histograms, win rate over time).
* Settings with gated mode toggle.
* **Done when:** Browser stays connected 24 hours without reconnect glitches; every panel shows live data; mode toggle correctly gated.

#### Phase 4 — Live trading (gated)

**Validation criteria — all must hold within last 14 days to unlock live:**

* ≥ 14 consecutive days of paper running.
* ≥ 400 paper trades executed.
* Net paper PnL after simulated fees + slippage > **+3 %** on bankroll.
* Paper Sharpe-like (per-day net return / std-dev over the 14 days) > **0.5**.
* Max paper daily drawdown ≤ **8 %**.
* Zero "ghost" trades (no trade in ledger without a `leader_trade_detected` parent).
* Zero unrecovered crash incidents.

Live milestones:
1. Run `set_allowances.py` on Polygon.
2. Mint L2 credentials via `bootstrap.py`.
3. Fund the Safe proxy with $500 USDC on Polygon.
4. Enable LIVE in dashboard settings; paper keeps running in parallel.
5. **First 72 h: half-size live** (sizing multiplier 0.5×) with manual monitoring of every trade.
6. After 72 h with no anomalies: full live sizing.
7. **Done when:** 30 days live, live PnL within 1σ of paper PnL over the same period.

---

### 10. Risk & failure modes

#### 10.1 Operational risks

* **API outages.** Every API call wrapped in `tenacity` retry with exponential backoff + jitter. After 3 retries, pause *new openings* but continue honoring *exits*.
* **WebSocket disconnects.** Exponential reconnect (1, 2, 4, 8, …, capped 60 s), re-subscribe, re-fetch book. Every reconnect logged.
* **RPC failures.** Multi-provider failover: Alchemy → Ankr → `polygon-rpc.com`. Health-check every 30 s; swap primary if Alchemy 5xx-rate > 10 % in last 5 min.
* **Subgraph lag.** Goldsky lags chain tip 5–30 s; subgraph data never used for trading decisions, only ranking and audit. Lag > 5 min surfaces in system health.
* **Polymarket chain migration risk.** April 2026 VP-Eng statement confirmed chain migration as a roadmap item. Every chain-specific constant lives behind a `Chain` config; a switch is a config + endpoint change, not a rewrite.

#### 10.2 Strategy / market risks (honest)

* **Smart-money wallets going cold.** Cold-wallet rule (§2.6) + daily re-ranking + per-leader attribution surface this fast.
* **Adverse selection.** You are by definition last to the trade; leader's edge decays in proportion to copy-volume. The +2-tick FOK price cap and aggregation cap mitigate. Expected live slippage relative to paper: ~30–80 bps in fast markets — reflected in the realistic paper pessimism.
* **Fees + slippage on a thin bankroll.** $500 × ~5 trades/day ≈ $25/day flow; spread costs of 50–200 bps per round-trip = $0.50–$1.00/day implicit cost. Year-long profitability requires leader net edge to clear ~5–10 % net of costs. Per Andrey Sergeenkov's April 6, 2026 on-chain analysis via Dune Analytics covering 2.5 million addresses on Polygon, 15.9 % of traders are in profit; only 2 % have lifetime profits above $1,000; and only 0.033 % (840 addresses) have earned more than $100,000. Profitability is the exception, not the norm, and the bot must be measured against that base rate.
* **Resolution risk.** Markets can resolve against an apparently-correct directional read. The §3.5 "6 hours to resolution" exit forces position close-out before resolution exposure.
* **Wash-trading contamination.** The antigaming layer catches obvious patterns; novel ones will slip through. Weekly manual review of top-contributing leaders is in `OPS.md`.

#### 10.3 Security risks

* **Private key exposure.** Default: key in OS keychain via Python `keyring` (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager / Linux Secret Service). The `.env` carries only the keychain reference, not the key. File mode 600 on `.env`, gitignored.
* **Stretch:** hardware wallet via `web3.py`'s Ledger middleware or a custom signer. Hardware signing adds ~500–800 ms per order — within the 2-second target. Optional but documented.
* **Logging redaction.** structlog has a serializer that redacts substrings `key`, `private_key`, `secret`, `passphrase`, `mnemonic`. Never logs the key.
* **Local network surface.** FastAPI binds to `127.0.0.1` only; dashboard on `localhost`. Remote access via `ssh -L` port-forwarding, never by exposing FastAPI.
* **Dependency supply chain.** `uv` and `pnpm` verify hashes; lockfiles committed; Dependabot enabled.

#### 10.4 Compliance & ToS

The bot does not bypass any geofencing. The user is responsible for being in a permitted jurisdiction and trading permitted contract types. International Polymarket reopened to US users in Feb 2026, but state-level restrictions apply to specific contract types — confirm before going live.

---

### 11. Free APIs & resources — exhaustive list

| API / resource | URL | Use in bot | Rate limit | Auth |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Polymarket CLOB | `https://clob.polymarket.com` | Orders, market data, tick sizes | 9,000/10s general; POST `/order` 3,500/10s burst, 36,000/10min | L1 + L2 |
| CLOB WS market | `wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/market` | Live books for simulator + display | ~5 concurrent WS/IP | None |
| CLOB WS user | `wss://ws-subscriptions-clob.polymarket.com/ws/user` | OUR own fills/orders | Same | L2 |
| Polymarket Gamma | `https://gamma-api.polymarket.com` | Market metadata, slug resolution | 4,000/10s; events 500/10s; markets 300/10s | None |
| Polymarket Data API | `https://data-api.polymarket.com` | Leader trades, positions, leaderboard | 1,000/10s; `/trades` 200/10s; `/positions` 150/10s | None |
| Data API leaderboard | `https://data-api.polymarket.com/v1/leaderboard` | Daily wallet seeding | included above | None |
| Goldsky positions subgraph | `…/positions-subgraph/0.0.7/gn` | Cross-validate positions, time series | Public free | None |
| Goldsky orderbook subgraph | `…/orderbook-subgraph/0.0.1/gn` | Historical fills | Public free | None |
| Goldsky activity subgraph | `…/activity-subgraph/0.0.4/gn` | Splits/merges/redemptions | Public free | None |
| Goldsky OI subgraph | `…/oi-subgraph/0.0.6/gn` | Liquidity gating | Public free | None |
| Goldsky PnL subgraph | `…/pnl-subgraph/0.0.14/gn` | Realized PnL for ranking | Public free | None |
| The Graph Polymarket | `gateway.thegraph.com/api/<key>/subgraphs/id/Bx1W4S7kDVxs9gC3s2G6DS8kdNBJNVhMviCtin2DiBp` | Backup if Goldsky outage | 100k queries/month free | Free API key |
| Alchemy Polygon free | `https://polygon-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/<KEY>` | Primary RPC + log subscription on Exchange V2 | 30 million compute units/month free, 25/s burst | Free API key |
| Alchemy WSS | `wss://polygon-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/<KEY>` | `eth_subscribe("logs", …)` | Same bucket | Free API key |
| Ankr public Polygon | `https://rpc.ankr.com/polygon` | RPC failover #2 | 200 million API credits/month freemium; 30 RPS public cap | None for public; key optional |
| Polygon public RPC | `https://polygon-rpc.com` | RPC failover #3 | Best-effort, no SLA | None |
| Chainlist | `https://chainlist.org/chain/137` | Discover more fallbacks | n/a | None |
| Polygonscan | `https://api.polygonscan.com/api` | Tx receipts, audit | 5 calls/s free | Free API key |
| CoinGecko Free | `https://api.coingecko.com/api/v3` | USDC reference price sanity check | 30 calls/min free | None |
| Dune Analytics free | `https://api.dune.com/api/v1/query/<id>/results` | Custom cross-validation | 2,500 credits/month (~500 query executions); pay-as-you-go at $5.00 per 100 additional credits beyond the bucket | Free API key |
| Polymarket leaderboard HTML | `https://polymarket.com/leaderboard/overall/monthly/profit` | Manual sanity-check | n/a | None |

**Optional / secondary (third-party views, not for programmatic ingestion):** `polymarketanalytics.com`, `polymarketrankings.com`, `polycopy.app`, `stand.trade`, `polytrackhq.app`, `merlin.trade/leaderboard`. **Optional Python wrapper:** the community `polymarket-apis` PyPI package (Pydantic-typed Gamma/Data/Subgraph/Web3/WebSocket clients) — viable alternative to building clients from scratch.

---

### 12. Appendix — decisions explicitly made (no "either/or")

| Decision | Choice | Rejected |
|---|---|---|
| Polymarket SDK | `py-clob-client-v2` | v1 (deprecated April 2026) |
| Wallet type | EOA + Gnosis Safe proxy (sigtype 2) | Magic-link (sigtype 1, harder to automate); pure EOA (sigtype 0, must pay gas every trade) |
| Detection path | Data API polling + Polygon log subscription (hybrid) | Subgraph-only (too laggy); user WS (only own trades) |
| Sizing | Fixed-dollar with rank multiplier | Proportional-to-leader (noisy estimation) |
| Order type | FOK market at +2 ticks | GTC limit (adverse-selection risk) |
| Aggregation | Single combined order on contemporaneous signals | N separate orders (slippage compounds) |
| Conflict resolution | First-mover or higher-score on simultaneous | Vote / average / pick larger leader |
| Storage | SQLite WAL + DuckDB for analytics | Postgres (over-engineered locally) |
| Paper / live | Two parallel ledger schemas | Single table with mode tag |
| Dashboard framework | Next.js + shadcn + Lightweight Charts | Streamlit / Dash / Tauri / SvelteKit |
| Logging | structlog + JSON → SQLite | Loki/Grafana (overkill) |
| Metrics | SQL on SQLite → JSON | Prometheus + Grafana (overkill) |
| Primary RPC | Alchemy Polygon free | Ankr (#2); public Polygon RPC (#3) |
| Key storage | OS keychain via `keyring` | Plain `.env` (insufficient); HSM (too heavy) |
| Concurrency | asyncio | Threads (GIL); multiprocessing (state-sharing pain) |
| Live unlock | 14-day, 400-trade, +3% paper PnL gate | Manual flip (too risky); no gate (irresponsible) |

---

### 13. What's NOT in scope

* Strategy / alpha design — by user mandate, the bot copies, it does not invent.
* Market making, arbitrage, RFQ — different domain.
* Multi-account fan-out / running for other users.
* Tax accounting beyond raw CSV export of fills.
* Telegram or mobile notifications — the local dashboard is the only UI.

---

## Recommendations

**Immediate (days 1–7).** Build the data layer first (Phase 1). Specifically: pin `py-clob-client-v2`, stand up the SQLite schema with WAL + migrations, implement read-only clients for the 5 Goldsky subgraphs and the three Polymarket APIs, and run the daily ranking job end-to-end. Verify wash-trading detection on 5 known leaderboard wallets (manually audit each result). **Do not write any order-placement code in this phase** — keep the bot read-only until ranking is trustworthy.

**Short term (weeks 2–4).** Phase 2 paper engine. The first integration test that matters is `test_paper_e2e.py`: take a recorded leader trade event, run it through detection → aggregation → sizing → fill simulator → ledger, and assert the full trace chain. The second is `test_crash_recovery.py`: `kill -9` mid-trade, restart, assert zero double-orders.

**Medium term (weeks 5–8).** Phase 3 dashboard. Build the Overview + Positions + Live Log views first; everything else is iterative polish. The mode toggle stays disabled in code until Phase 4.

**Live unlock decision.** Do not flip to live until ALL §9 criteria are satisfied. If 14 days of paper produces less than +3 % net PnL, that is the data telling you the leader-selection or sizing is not yet good enough; iterate, do not flip. **If you flip to live and the first week diverges from paper by more than 1σ, revert to paper.** This is a feature of the architecture (two-ledger setup means switching is one config flag), not a failure.

**Benchmarks that would change recommendations:**

* If Polymarket's chain migration is announced with a date, immediately re-evaluate the RPC layer and confirm Alchemy/Ankr support the destination chain. If they don't, the project is paused until they do — chain-only reads are non-negotiable.
* If Polymarket deprecates the unauthenticated Data API or the public Goldsky subgraphs, switch to The Graph Network's hosted subgraph (100k queries/month free with API key) and prepare to budget for paid tier if necessary.
* If `min_order_size` increases (e.g., to 10 shares), revisit the $5 base sizing — at $5/copy and a 10-share minimum, you'd need outcomes priced ≤ $0.50 only, which biases the bot toward one half of every market.
* If the user's bankroll grows beyond $5,000, switch sizing from fixed-dollar to a Kelly-fractional model — at that bankroll, per-trade dollar floors stop dominating.

---

## Caveats

* **The 25 % wash-trading figure refers to the historical aggregate, not necessarily the current period.** The Columbia / Barnard SSRN paper measured the prior three years; the share in any given week could be higher or lower. Treat it as a planning constant, not a real-time signal.
* **Latency targets are theoretical.** A 2-second end-to-end target assumes Alchemy's free tier responds in <200 ms and Polymarket's CLOB matches in <600 ms. Both can degrade during high-volume events (sports finals, election nights). The bot's behavior under such conditions is "trade fewer signals, more conservatively"; not "trade harder."
* **Polymarket's documented rate limits are confirmed via Cloudflare throttling**, but per-endpoint exact numbers (e.g., `/trades` at 200/10s) are reported by third-party reference guides citing the official rate-limit table; the bot enforces conservative client-side limits well below stated maxima and additionally honors `429` responses with backoff.
* **The CLOB V2 platform has been live only since April 28, 2026.** Subtle behavioral changes (e.g., fee at match time vs placement time) may produce unexpected ledger discrepancies relative to pre-V2 documentation written by third parties — verify against the official docs at `docs.polymarket.com` whenever in doubt.
* **Profitability is not promised.** The 15.9 %/2 %/0.033 % base rates from on-chain data on 2.5 million addresses tell you the field of which this bot is a member. Copy-trading shifts those odds in your favor only if the underlying leaders are durably skilled — which the daily re-ranking and antigaming layer are designed to maximize but cannot guarantee.
* **Polymarket's possible chain migration off Polygon could obsolete parts of this plan.** No timeline has been announced. Keep RPC, contract addresses, and chain ID behind configuration to make the migration a config diff rather than a rewrite.
* **Regulatory landscape is mid-flux.** Polymarket's CFTC posture, state-level lawsuits, and international bans are evolving month-to-month in 2026. Verify your jurisdiction's current status before depositing real funds; do not rely on this document's snapshot for legal advice.

— End of document.

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[project]
name = "polymarket-bot"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Polymarket copy-trading bot — paper + live, full stack"
requires-python = ">=3.12"
dependencies = [
    "httpx>=0.27.0",
    "websockets>=13.0",
    "gql[aiohttp]>=3.5.0",
    "web3>=7.0.0",
    "pydantic>=2.8.0",
    "pydantic-settings>=2.6.0",
    "structlog>=24.4.0",
    "fastapi>=0.115.0",
    "uvicorn[standard]>=0.32.0",
    "aiosqlite>=0.20.0",
    "duckdb>=1.1.0",
    "tenacity>=9.0.0",
    "keyring>=25.4.0",
    "eth-account>=0.13.0",
    "honcho>=2.0.0",
    "aiofiles>=24.1.0",
    "python-multipart>=0.0.9",
    "python-dateutil>=2.9.0",
    # Polymarket V2 SDK — install from GitHub if not on PyPI:
    # uv pip install "py-clob-client-v2 @ git+https://github.com/Polymarket/py-clob-client-v2"
    # Or comment out and install manually: pip install py-clob-client-v2
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    "ruff>=0.7.0",
    "pytest>=8.3.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=0.24.0",
    "httpx[test]>=0.27.0",
    "pytest-mock>=3.14.0",
]

[project.scripts]
polymarket-bot = "bot.main:cli"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["bot"]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py312"

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "UP", "B"]
ignore = ["E501"]

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
testpaths = ["tests"]

[tool.uv]
dev-dependencies = [
    "ruff>=0.7.0",

# ─── Polymarket Bot — .env.example ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Copy to .env and fill in your values.
# NEVER commit .env to git. Private key lives in OS keychain (see README).
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── Mode ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BOT_MODE=PAPER          # PAPER | LIVE  (dashboard Settings has a gated toggle)

# ── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DATABASE_PATH=bot.db    # relative path from project root; WAL mode is on by default

# ── Alchemy (Polygon free tier — 30M CU/month) ───────────────────────────────
ALCHEMY_API_KEY=your_alchemy_api_key_here
ALCHEMY_POLYGON_HTTP=https://polygon-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/${ALCHEMY_API_KEY}
ALCHEMY_POLYGON_WSS=wss://polygon-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/${ALCHEMY_API_KEY}

# ── RPC failover ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANKR_POLYGON_RPC=https://rpc.ankr.com/polygon
PUBLIC_POLYGON_RPC=https://polygon-rpc.com

# ── The Graph API key (backup if Goldsky outage) ─────────────────────────────
THE_GRAPH_API_KEY=       # optional; 100k queries/month free

# ── Dune Analytics (optional cross-validation) ───────────────────────────────
DUNE_API_KEY=            # optional; 2,500 credits/month free

# ── Polygonscan (optional tx audit) ──────────────────────────────────────────
POLYGONSCAN_API_KEY=     # optional; 5 calls/s free

# ── Keychain service names (values are stored IN the OS keychain, not here) ──
# The bot reads these strings and uses them as keyring service identifiers.
KEYRING_SERVICE_EOA_KEY=polymarket-bot-eoa-key
KEYRING_SERVICE_CLOB_API=polymarket-bot-clob-api
KEYRING_SERVICE_CLOB_SECRET=polymarket-bot-clob-secret
KEYRING_SERVICE_CLOB_PASSPHRASE=polymarket-bot-clob-passphrase

# ── Bot wallet (proxy address — public, safe to store here) ──────────────────
# Your Gnosis Safe proxy address (the funder). Derived automatically on first run.
BOT_PROXY_ADDRESS=       # e.g. 0x1234...abcd
BOT_EOA_ADDRESS=         # e.g. 0xabcd...1234

# ── Discovery / ranking ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROSTER_ACTIVE_SIZE=30           # wallets in active copy tier
ROSTER_STANDBY_SIZE=20          # wallets tracked but not copied
DISCOVERY_HOUR_UTC=4            # daily re-ranking job trigger hour (UTC)

# ── Sizing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PER_TRADE_BASE_USD=5.00
MIN_NOTIONAL_USD=5.00
MAX_NOTIONAL_USD=10.00

# ── Risk / kill-switch ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MAX_CONCURRENT_POSITIONS=25
MAX_EXPOSURE_PER_MARKET_USD=30.00
MAX_EXPOSURE_PER_LEADER_USD=50.00
MAX_DAILY_OPENINGS=40
DAILY_LOSS_KILL_SWITCH_USD=40.00    # 8% of $500 bankroll
PER_POSITION_STOP_LOSS_PCT=0.40     # -40% of notional

# ── Execution ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AGGREGATION_WINDOW_SECONDS=120
FOK_TICK_BUFFER=2                   # +N ticks above leader fill price for FOK buy
SIGNAL_STAGGER_SECONDS=3            # per-leader poll interval for Data API

# ── Paper trading simulator ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
PAPER_PESSIMISM_ADJUSTMENT_CENTS=0.5   # worst-side VWAP nudge
PAPER_DEPTH_HAIRCUT=0.25               # drop 25% visible book size
PAPER_FEE_RATE_BPS=0                   # current Polymarket taker fee

# ── FastAPI control plane ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
API_HOST=127.0.0.1
API_PORT=8787

# ── Chain / contract addresses (update if Polymarket migrates off Polygon) ───
CHAIN_ID=137
CLOB_EXCHANGE_V2=0x4bFb41d5B3570DeFd03C39a9A4D8dE6Bd8B8982E
CTF_EXCHANGE_V2=0x4bFb41d5B3570DeFd03C39a9A4D8dE6Bd8B8982E
PROXY_FACTORY=0x56C79347e95530c01A2FC76E732f9566dA16E113
PUSDC_ADDRESS=0x2791Bca1f2de4661ED88A30C99A7a9449Aa84174  # pUSD on Polygon

input.content:
"""
Shared data models used across all bot modules.
Import from here to avoid circular dependencies.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any


# ── Enums ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class OrderSide(str, Enum):
    BUY = "BUY"
    SELL = "SELL"


class OrderStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING = "PENDING"
    SUBMITTED = "SUBMITTED"
    FILLED = "FILLED"
    PARTIAL = "PARTIAL"
    CANCELED = "CANCELED"
    REJECTED = "REJECTED"


class SignalStatus(str, Enum):
    PENDING = "pending"
    EXECUTED = "executed"
    SKIPPED = "skipped"
    AGGREGATED = "aggregated"


class LeaderTier(str, Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    STANDBY = "standby"


class LeaderStatus(str, Enum):
    ACTIVE = "active"
    COLD = "cold"
    PAUSED = "paused"


# ── Market / book ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@dataclass
class BookLevel:
    price: float
    size: float


@dataclass
class OrderBook:
    token_id: str
    market_id: str
    bids: list[BookLevel]
    asks: list[BookLevel]
    updated_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)

    def best_ask(self) -> float | None:
        return self.asks[0].price if self.asks else None

    def best_bid(self) -> float | None:
        return self.bids[0].price if self.bids else None

    def mid(self) -> float | None:
        a, b = self.best_ask(), self.best_bid()
        if a is None or b is None:
            return None
        return (a + b) / 2


@dataclass
class MarketInfo:
    condition_id: str       # market ID
    yes_token_id: str
    no_token_id: str
    question: str
    end_date_iso: str
    tick_size: float
    min_order_size: float   # shares
    active: bool
    resolved: bool


# ── Leader signals ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@dataclass
class LeaderTrade:
    """A trade detected from a leader wallet."""
    leader_proxy: str
    market_id: str          # conditionId
    token_id: str           # assetId (YES or NO token)
    outcome: str            # "YES" | "NO"
    side: str               # "BUY" | "SELL"
    price: float
    size_shares: float
    notional_usd: float
    fill_tx_hash: str
    detected_at: datetime
    source: str             # "data_api" | "polygon_log" | "subgraph"
    trace_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))


@dataclass
class SignalEvent:
    """Normalized signal ready for aggregation."""
    trace_id: str
    leader_proxy: str
    leader_rank: int
    leader_score: float
    market_id: str
    token_id: str
    outcome: str            # "YES" | "NO"
    side: str               # "BUY" | "SELL"
    price: float
    notional_usd: float
    detected_at: datetime
    aggregation_key: str    # f"{market_id}:{outcome}:{window_bucket}"


# ── Order execution ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@dataclass
class OrderIntent:
    """Everything needed to execute one order."""
    trace_id: str
    leader_proxies: list[str]
    market_id: str
    token_id: str
    outcome: str
    side: str               # "BUY" | "SELL"
    size_shares: int
    price: float            # FOK limit price
    notional_usd: float
    client_order_id: str
    tick_size: float
    min_order_size: float


@dataclass
class FillResult:
    """Result from paper simulator or live CLOB."""
    client_order_id: str
    status: OrderStatus
    fill_price: float | None
    fill_size: float | None
    fees_usd: float
    slippage_bps: float
    rejected_reason: str | None
    server_order_id: str | None = None     # live only
    book_snapshot: dict[str, Any] | None = None  # paper only


# ── Positions ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@dataclass
class Position:
    """An open or closed position in either ledger."""
    position_id: int
    market_id: str
    token_id: str
    outcome: str
    opened_at: datetime
    entry_price: float
    mark_price: float
    size_shares: float
    notional_usd: float
    leader_proxies: list[str]
    is_open: bool
    closed_at: datetime | None = None
    exit_reason: str | None = None
    realized_pnl: float | None = None


# ── Leaders ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@dataclass
class LeaderCandidate:
    """Raw candidate from the discovery funnel."""
    proxy_address: str
    trades_30d: int
    trade_freq: float
    win_rate: float
    realized_pnl_30d: float
    avg_position_usd: float
    per_trade_pnl: float
    per_trade_pnl_std: float
    sharpe_like: float
    market_diversity: float
    recent_7d_pnl: float
    median_hold_hours: float
    wash_score: float
    last_trade_ts: int


@dataclass
class Leader:
    """Scored, ranked leader on the active roster."""
    proxy_address: str
    rank: int
    tier: LeaderTier
    score: float
    score_delta: float
    status: LeaderStatus
    cold_since_ts: int | None
    snapshot_date: str      # YYYY-MM-DD

"""
UUIDv7 (time-sortable) trace IDs + structlog context vars.
Every `leader_trade_detected` event creates a new trace_id that threads
through all downstream events — detection → signal → order → fill.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import time
import uuid
from contextvars import ContextVar

import structlog

_trace_id_var: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("trace_id", default="")
_span_id_var: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("span_id", default="")


def _uuid7() -> str:
    """Generate a UUIDv7: 48-bit ms timestamp + random. Time-sortable."""
    ms = int(time.time() * 1000)
    rand = uuid.uuid4().int & ((1 << 74) - 1)
    # Layout: 48b unix_ms | 4b ver=7 | 12b rand_a | 2b var | 62b rand_b
    val = (ms << 80) | (0x7 << 76) | ((rand >> 62) << 64) | (0b10 << 62) | (rand & ((1 << 62) - 1))
    return str(uuid.UUID(int=val))


def new_trace() -> str:
    tid = _uuid7()
    _trace_id_var.set(tid)
    _span_id_var.set(_uuid7())
    return tid


def new_span() -> str:
    sid = _uuid7()
    _span_id_var.set(sid)
    return sid


def current_trace() -> str:
    return _trace_id_var.get()


def current_span() -> str:
    return _span_id_var.get()


def inject_trace_context(logger, method_name, event_dict):
    """structlog processor: adds trace_id and span_id to every log record."""
    tid = _trace_id_var.get()
    sid = _span_id_var.get()
    if tid:
        event_dict["trace_id"] = tid
    if sid:
        event_dict["span_id"] = sid
    return event_dict

"""
structlog configuration: structured JSON to stdout + daily-rotating file + SQLite events table.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import logging.handlers
import re
from pathlib import Path

import structlog

from bot.observability.trace import inject_trace_context

_REDACT_PATTERN = re.compile(
    r"(private_key|key|secret|passphrase|mnemonic|api_key)", re.IGNORECASE
)
_INITIALIZED = False


def _redact(logger, method_name, event_dict: dict) -> dict:
    """Strip sensitive substrings from log payloads."""
    for k in list(event_dict.keys()):
        if _REDACT_PATTERN.search(k):
            event_dict[k] = "***REDACTED***"
    return event_dict


def configure_logging(log_dir: Path | None = None, level: str = "INFO") -> None:
    global _INITIALIZED
    if _INITIALIZED:
        return
    _INITIALIZED = True

    handlers: list[logging.Handler] = [logging.StreamHandler()]

    if log_dir:
        log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        file_handler = logging.handlers.TimedRotatingFileHandler(
            log_dir / "bot.log",
            when="midnight",
            utc=True,
            backupCount=30,
        )
        handlers.append(file_handler)

    logging.basicConfig(
        level=getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO),
        format="%(message)s",
        handlers=handlers,
    )

    structlog.configure(
        processors=[
            structlog.contextvars.merge_contextvars,
            inject_trace_context,
            structlog.stdlib.add_log_level,
            structlog.stdlib.add_logger_name,
            structlog.processors.TimeStamper(fmt="iso"),
            _redact,
            structlog.processors.StackInfoRenderer(),
            structlog.processors.ExceptionRenderer(),
            structlog.processors.JSONRenderer(),
        ],
        wrapper_class=structlog.make_filtering_bound_logger(
            getattr(logging, level.upper(), logging.INFO)
        ),
        context_class=dict,
        logger_factory=structlog.stdlib.LoggerFactory(),
        cache_logger_on_first_use=True,
    )


def get_logger(name: str) -> structlog.BoundLogger:
    return structlog.get_logger(name)
